title: Rishikesh 7-Day Adventure Tour
metaTitle: Rishikesh 7-Day Adventure Tour | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 7-day trip to Rishikesh, India: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Ganges white-water rafting (Shivpuri to Rishikesh) and Kaudiyala rafting stretch. Prices from $1374 per person (estimate).
overview: Spend 7 days discovering Rishikesh, India — Foothill climate milder than the plains below: warm summers (highs 32-36°C in May-June), a wet monsoon June-September that raises the Ganges, and cool, pleasant winters (highs 18-20°C, chilly nights near 5-8°C). September to April for pleasant trekking and rafting conditions (highs 20-30°C); the Ganges is at its highest and most thrilling for rafting just after monsoon (Sep-Oct). This itinerary blends Ganges white-water rafting (Shivpuri to Rishikesh), Kaudiyala rafting stretch, Laxman Jhula with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
6 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Ganges white-water rafting (Shivpuri to Rishikesh), Kaudiyala rafting stretch, Laxman Jhula
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Rishikesh
itinerary.description: Land at Dehradun (Jolly Grant) Airport, nearest gateway (DED) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Ganges white-water rafting (Shivpuri to Rishikesh) — Grade II-III rapids on the Ganges, the region's signature activity. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Rishikesh: Kaudiyala rafting stretch & Bungee jumping, Jumpin Heights
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Kaudiyala rafting stretch — Longer, wilder rapids further upstream for experienced rafters. In the afternoon, continue to Bungee jumping, Jumpin Heights — India's highest fixed bungee platform, near Mohanchatti. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Rishikesh: Giant swing and flying fox, Jumpin Heights & The Beatles Ashram (Chaurasi Kutia)
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Giant swing and flying fox, Jumpin Heights — Additional adventure activities at the same adventure park. In the afternoon, continue to The Beatles Ashram (Chaurasi Kutia) — Abandoned ashram where the Beatles wrote much of the White Album, now covered in murals. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.
activities: Giant swing and flying fox, Jumpin Heights

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Haridwar
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Haridwar, a popular side trip from Rishikesh. Return to Rishikesh in the evening with time free to explore on your own.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include The Beatles Ashram murals — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Rishikesh: Neer Garh Waterfall entry & Laxman Jhula
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Neer Garh Waterfall entry — Multi-tier waterfall a short trek from Laxman Jhula. In the afternoon, continue to Laxman Jhula — Iconic pedestrian suspension bridge over the Ganges. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Ram Jhula or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to DED for your departure flight.

travelTips: Book rafting through a licensed operator with certified guides and life jackets — check reviews, not just roadside stalls.
September-October, right after monsoon, offers the biggest and most exciting rapids for rafting.
Attend the Parmarth Niketan evening aarti early to get a front-row seat by the fire pits.
Many ashorams offer short-term yoga and meditation courses — book ahead during the March International Yoga Festival.
Alcohol and meat are officially banned within Rishikesh municipal limits — don't expect bars in town.
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Modest, loose-fitting clothing suitable for yoga and ashram visits
Quick-dry clothes and sandals for rafting
A light jacket for cool riverside evenings
Reusable water bottle — plastic bottles are discouraged near the river
thingsToDo: Giant swing and flying fox, Jumpin Heights
Bungee jumping and giant swing at Jumpin Heights
Cliff jumping and camping on Ganges riverside beaches
Gentle rafting stretch (Grade I-II) suitable for children
thingsToAvoid: Unlicensed rafting operators without visible safety gear or trained guides.
Swimming alone in the Ganges — currents are stronger than they appear, especially post-monsoon.
Overpaying inflated "donation" requests from self-appointed sadhus at the ghats.
Walking on suspension bridges during festival crowds — Laxman Jhula in particular can get dangerously packed.
Bringing alcohol or meat into ashram accommodations — it can get you asked to leave.
topRestaurants: Little Buddha Cafe — Cafe & Continental, riverside ($$)
shoppingAreas: Swarg Ashram bazaar (malas, singing bowls, yoga wear)
Tibetan market stalls near Ram Jhula
nightlife: Evening Ganga Aarti at Parmarth Niketan Ashram
Evening Ganga Aarti at Triveni Ghat
Riverside bonfire and satsang sessions at ashrams
Live acoustic music at riverside cafes

Riverside candlelight dinner at a boutique resort
Evening aarti followed by a quiet riverside stroll

Budget ashram stay with included yoga classes

Shared-cost rafting trip with a small group

festivalCalendar.name: Kumbh Mela (nearby Haridwar)
festivalCalendar.timing: Once every 12 years, with smaller Ardh Kumbh every 6 years

localEtiquette: Rishikesh is officially vegetarian and alcohol-free within municipal limits — avoid bringing meat or alcohol into ashrams and much of the town.
Dress modestly, especially around ashrams and temples — shoulders and knees covered.
Remove shoes before entering ashrams, yoga halls and temples.
Keep noise levels down near ashrams and the riverbanks, which are considered meditative spaces.
Ask before photographing sadhus (holy men) — a small donation is customary if they agree.
localTransportGuide: Rishikesh is walkable along the two main riverside hubs (Laxman Jhula and Ram Jhula), connected by shared autos and e-rickshaws; scooters are popular rentals for reaching Neelkanth Temple or waterfalls. Vehicles cannot cross the pedestrian suspension bridges, so plan foot time on both banks.
bestTimeToVisit: September to April for pleasant trekking and rafting conditions (highs 20-30°C); the Ganges is at its highest and most thrilling for rafting just after monsoon (Sep-Oct).
weather: Foothill climate milder than the plains below: warm summers (highs 32-36°C in May-June), a wet monsoon June-September that raises the Ganges, and cool, pleasant winters (highs 18-20°C, chilly nights near 5-8°C).
visaInformation: Most foreign nationals require an Indian e-Visa applied for online before travel. Indian citizens need no visa, and OCI/PIO cardholders travel visa-free with a linked foreign passport — relevant for the many diaspora visitors who come specifically for yoga teacher-training programs.
averageFlightTime: ~9.5h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Rishikesh?
faqs.answer: Most foreign nationals require an Indian e-Visa applied for online before travel. Indian citizens need no visa, and OCI/PIO cardholders travel visa-free with a linked foreign passport — relevant for the many diaspora visitors who come specifically for yoga teacher-training programs.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Rishikesh?
faqs.answer: September to April for pleasant trekking and rafting conditions (highs 20-30°C); the Ganges is at its highest and most thrilling for rafting just after monsoon (Sep-Oct). Foothill climate milder than the plains below: warm summers (highs 32-36°C in May-June), a wet monsoon June-September that raises the Ganges, and cool, pleasant winters (highs 18-20°C, chilly nights near 5-8°C).

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Rishikesh safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Rishikesh is generally safe for tourists who take standard precautions — avoid poorly lit areas at night, keep valuables secure, and use licensed transport.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Rishikesh?
faqs.answer: The local currency is INR. Carry some cash for smaller vendors — card acceptance can be inconsistent outside major hotels and malls.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.

reviews.author: Mia Nguyen
reviews.title: Great for families with kids
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Swarg Ashram area and Parmarth Niketan Ashram. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Yuki Wang
reviews.title: Loved every moment
reviews.body: Swarg Ashram area alone was worth the trip, but combined with Parmarth Niketan Ashram this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Isabella Larsen
reviews.title: Amazing itinerary, zero stress
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Neelkanth Mahadev Temple and Kaudiyala rafting stretch. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: David Wang
reviews.title: Highly recommend this package
reviews.body: Rishikesh is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially The Beatles Ashram (Chaurasi Kutia). The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Sofia Brown
reviews.title: So many hidden gems included
reviews.body: Booked this as a solo trip and it was well paced. The Beatles Ashram (Chaurasi Kutia) in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Mohammed Khan
reviews.title: A little rushed but still wonderful
reviews.body: Ram Jhula alone was worth the trip, but combined with Triveni Ghat this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.
